mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier discounting and improves equity valuation, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is still declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average during months with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, higher real funding costs and tighter financial conditions compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average 20d return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike triggers are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect vanishes after rolling 3-year walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS relative to its recent monthly trend indicates weaker consumer spending and revenue growth, which should pressure equity prices and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if downward RSAFS trend months do not coincide with below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the signal's rank correlation with forward returns is near zero over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while HOUST is also rising, improving industrial output and housing activity indicate synchronized real-sector expansion, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in INDPRO and HOUST does not outperform each series alone in predicting 60d SPX returns, or if triggered periods fail to show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent level reflects weakening residential demand and tighter credit transmission, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days when housing starts break down.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start breakdowns are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is absent after excluding outlier months.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-softening regime, rising PAYEMS combined with stable or falling UNRATE indicates expanding payrolls without unemployment pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS/UNRATE combination does not improve predictive accuracy versus PAYEMS alone, or if positive 20d returns are not more frequent when payrolls rise and unemployment does not.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate together with accelerating CPIAUCSL implies higher real-rate pressure and multiple compression, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation periods do not produce below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in a majority of rolling 3-year windows.
